Code:
fn process_create_associated_token_account(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    create_mode: CreateMode,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let account_info_iter = &mut accounts.iter();
    let funder_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let associated_token_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let wallet_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let spl_token_mint_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let system_program_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let spl_token_program_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let spl_token_program_id = spl_token_program_info.key;

    let (associated_token_address, bump_seed) = get_associated_token_address_and_bump_seed_internal(
        wallet_account_info.key,
        spl_token_mint_info.key,
        program_id,
        spl_token_program_id,
    );
    if associated_token_address != *associated_token_account_info.key {
        msg!("Error: Associated address does not match seed derivation");
        return Err(ProgramError::InvalidSeeds);
    }

    let associated_token_account_signer_seeds: &[&[_]] = &[
        &wallet_account_info.key.to_bytes(),
        &spl_token_program_id.to_bytes(),
        &spl_token_mint_info.key.to_bytes(),
        &[bump_seed],
    ];

    create_pda_account(
        funder_info,
        &Rent::get()?,
        account_len,
        spl_token_program_id,
        system_program_info,
        associated_token_account_info,
        associated_token_account_signer_seeds,
    )?;

    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V9 - Bump Seed Canonicalization - MITIGATED
The function derives the canonical PDA using get_associated_token_address_and_bump_seed_internal() which internally calls find_program_address(). The derived address is verified against the provided account. The canonical bump is then used for signing the account creation.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept PDA derivation and verification logic from lines 82-93

fn process_create_associated_token_account(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    bump_seed: u8,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let account_info_iter = &mut accounts.iter();
    let funder_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let associated_token_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let wallet_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let spl_token_mint_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let system_program_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let spl_token_program_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let spl_token_program_id = spl_token_program_info.key;

    let associated_token_address = Pubkey::create_program_address(
        &[
            &wallet_account_info.key.to_bytes(),
            &spl_token_program_id.to_bytes(),
            &spl_token_mint_info.key.to_bytes(),
            &[bump_seed],
        ],
        program_id,
    )?;
    if associated_token_address != *associated_token_account_info.key {
        return Err(ProgramError::InvalidSeeds);
    }

    let associated_token_account_signer_seeds: &[&[_]] = &[
        &wallet_account_info.key.to_bytes(),
        &spl_token_program_id.to_bytes(),
        &spl_token_mint_info.key.to_bytes(),
        &[bump_seed],
    ];

    create_pda_account(
        funder_info,
        &Rent::get()?,
        account_len,
        spl_token_program_id,
        system_program_info,
        associated_token_account_info,
        associated_token_account_signer_seeds,
    )?;

    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V9 - Bump Seed Canonicalization
HIGH
Accepts user-provided bump_seed parameter and uses create_program_address() instead of the original get_associated_token_address_and_bump_seed_internal() which derives canonical bump. An attacker could create multiple token accounts for the same wallet/mint pair with different non-canonical bumps.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - replaced get_associated_token_address_and_bump_seed_internal (L82, uses find_program_address) with create_program_address accepting user-provided bump_seed parameter

Code:
pub fn process_recover_nested(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    let account_info_iter = &mut accounts.iter();
    let nested_associated_token_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let nested_token_mint_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let destination_associated_token_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let owner_associated_token_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let owner_token_mint_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let wallet_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let spl_token_program_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let spl_token_program_id = spl_token_program_info.key;

    let (owner_associated_token_address, bump_seed) =
        get_associated_token_address_and_bump_seed_internal(
            wallet_account_info.key,
            owner_token_mint_info.key,
            program_id,
            spl_token_program_id,
        );
    if owner_associated_token_address != *owner_associated_token_account_info.key {
        return Err(ProgramError::InvalidSeeds);
    }

    let (nested_associated_token_address, _) = get_associated_token_address_and_bump_seed_internal(
        owner_associated_token_account_info.key,
        nested_token_mint_info.key,
        program_id,
        spl_token_program_id,
    );
    if nested_associated_token_address != *nested_associated_token_account_info.key {
        return Err(ProgramError::InvalidSeeds);
    }

    let (destination_associated_token_address, _) =
        get_associated_token_address_and_bump_seed_internal(
            wallet_account_info.key,
            nested_token_mint_info.key,
            program_id,
            spl_token_program_id,
        );
    if destination_associated_token_address != *destination_associated_token_account_info.key {
        return Err(ProgramError::InvalidSeeds);
    }

    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V9 - Bump Seed Canonicalization - MITIGATED
Triple PDA verification: owner, nested, and destination addresses are all derived using get_associated_token_address_and_bump_seed_internal() (which uses find_program_address) and verified against provided accounts. No user-provided bumps accepted.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept triple PDA verification from lines 161-200

Code:
pub fn process_recover_nested(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    let account_info_iter = &mut accounts.iter();
    let nested_associated_token_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let nested_token_mint_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let destination_associated_token_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let owner_associated_token_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let owner_token_mint_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let wallet_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let spl_token_program_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let spl_token_program_id = spl_token_program_info.key;

    if !wallet_account_info.is_signer {
        msg!("Wallet of the owner associated token account must sign");
        return Err(ProgramError::MissingRequiredSignature);
    }

    if owner_token_mint_info.owner != spl_token_program_id {
        return Err(ProgramError::IllegalOwner);
    }

    if owner_associated_token_account_info.owner != spl_token_program_id {
        return Err(ProgramError::IllegalOwner);
    }
    let owner_account_data = owner_associated_token_account_info.data.borrow();
    let owner_account = StateWithExtensions::<Account>::unpack(&owner_account_data)?;
    if owner_account.base.owner != *wallet_account_info.key {
        return Err(AssociatedTokenAccountError::InvalidOwner.into());
    }

    if nested_associated_token_account_info.owner != spl_token_program_id {
        return Err(ProgramError::IllegalOwner);
    }
    let nested_account_data = nested_associated_token_account_info.data.borrow();
    let nested_account = StateWithExtensions::<Account>::unpack(&nested_account_data)?;
    if nested_account.base.owner != *owner_associated_token_account_info.key {
        return Err(AssociatedTokenAccountError::InvalidOwner.into());
    }

    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V1 - Access Control - MITIGATED
The function verifies: (1) wallet must sign the transaction, (2) owner mint owned by token program, (3) owner ATA owned by token program with correct wallet owner, (4) nested ATA owned by token program with correct owner ATA as owner. Complete ownership chain validation.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept signer check (L203) and ownership chain verification (L208-243)

pub fn process_recover_nested(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    let account_info_iter = &mut accounts.iter();
    let nested_associated_token_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let nested_token_mint_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let destination_associated_token_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let owner_associated_token_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let owner_token_mint_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let wallet_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let spl_token_program_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let spl_token_program_id = spl_token_program_info.key;

    let nested_account_data = nested_associated_token_account_info.data.borrow();
    let nested_account = StateWithExtensions::<Account>::unpack(&nested_account_data)?;
    let amount = nested_account.base.amount;

    let nested_mint_data = nested_token_mint_info.data.borrow();
    let nested_mint = StateWithExtensions::<Mint>::unpack(&nested_mint_data)?;
    let decimals = nested_mint.base.decimals;

    invoke_signed(
        &spl_token_2022_interface::instruction::transfer_checked(
            spl_token_program_id,
            nested_associated_token_account_info.key,
            nested_token_mint_info.key,
            destination_associated_token_account_info.key,
            owner_associated_token_account_info.key,
            &[],
            amount,
            decimals,
        )?,
        &[nested_associated_token_account_info.clone(), nested_token_mint_info.clone(),
          destination_associated_token_account_info.clone(), owner_associated_token_account_info.clone()],
        &[owner_associated_token_account_signer_seeds],
    )?;

    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V1 - Access Control
CRITICAL
The function does not verify that wallet_account_info is a signer and does not validate the ownership chain. Anyone can trigger recovery of nested tokens to an arbitrary destination without proving they own the wallet.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - removed wallet is_signer check (L203-206), removed all owner verification checks (L208-243), removed mint ownership check (L207)

You are a Solana smart contract security analyzer. Analyze the following Rust code for vulnerabilities.

Code:
fn process_create_associated_token_account(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
) -> ProgramResult {
    let account_info_iter = &mut accounts.iter();
    let funder_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let associated_token_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let wallet_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let spl_token_mint_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let spl_token_program_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;

    create_pda_account(
        funder_info, &Rent::get()?, account_len,
        spl_token_program_info.key, system_program_info,
        associated_token_account_info, associated_token_account_signer_seeds,
    )?;

    invoke(
        &spl_token_2022_interface::instruction::initialize_immutable_owner(
            spl_token_program_info.key,
            associated_token_account_info.key,
        )?,
        &[associated_token_account_info.clone(), spl_token_program_info.clone()],
    )?;

    invoke(
        &spl_token_2022_interface::instruction::initialize_account3(
            spl_token_program_info.key,
            associated_token_account_info.key,
            spl_token_mint_info.key,
            wallet_account_info.key,
        )?,
        &[associated_token_account_info.clone(), spl_token_mint_info.clone(),
          wallet_account_info.clone(), spl_token_program_info.clone()],
    )
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V6 - Unchecked External Calls - MITIGATED
All three CPIs use proper error propagation: create_pda_account uses ?, initialize_immutable_owner invoke uses ?, and initialize_account3 invoke returns its Result directly. If any step fails, the entire transaction reverts atomically.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept CPI chain with error propagation

    invoke_signed(

text:
<|begin_of_text|><|start_header_id|>system<|end_header_id|>

You are a Solana smart contract security analyzer. Analyze code for vulnerabilities according to OWASP Top 10 for Blockchain. Respond with the vulnerability status and details.<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>user<|end_header_id|>

You are a Solana smart contract security analyzer. Analyze the following Rust code for vulnerabilities.

Code:
pub fn process_recover_nested(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    let account_info_iter = &mut accounts.iter();
    let nested_associated_token_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let destination_associated_token_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let owner_associated_token_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let wallet_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let spl_token_program_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;

    if !wallet_account_info.is_signer {
        return Err(ProgramError::MissingRequiredSignature);
    }

    let (amount, decimals) = {
        let nested_account_data = nested_associated_token_account_info.data.borrow();
        let nested_account = StateWithExtensions::<Account>::unpack(&nested_account_data)?;
        let amount = nested_account.base.amount;
        let nested_mint_data = nested_token_mint_info.data.borrow();
        let nested_mint = StateWithExtensions::<Mint>::unpack(&nested_mint_data)?;
        (amount, nested_mint.base.decimals)
    };

    // Transfer everything out FIRST
    invoke_signed(
        &spl_token_2022_interface::instruction::transfer_checked(
            spl_token_program_id, nested_associated_token_account_info.key,
            nested_token_mint_info.key, destination_associated_token_account_info.key,
            owner_associated_token_account_info.key, &[], amount, decimals,
        )?,
        &[nested_associated_token_account_info.clone(), nested_token_mint_info.clone(),
          destination_associated_token_account_info.clone(), owner_associated_token_account_info.clone()],
        &[owner_associated_token_account_signer_seeds],
    )?;

    // THEN close the account so it can never be used again
    invoke_signed(
        &spl_token_2022_interface::instruction::close_account(
            spl_token_program_id, nested_associated_token_account_info.key,
            wallet_account_info.key, owner_associated_token_account_info.key, &[],
        )?,
        &[nested_associated_token_account_info.clone(), wallet_account_info.clone(),
          owner_associated_token_account_info.clone()],
        &[owner_associated_token_account_signer_seeds],
    )
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V5 - Reentrancy Attacks - MITIGATED
Transfer executes before close. Even with a reentrancy attempt during transfer, the nested account still exists with its original balance. After successful transfer, the account is closed permanently, preventing any future recovery attempts on the same nested account.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept transfer-then-close CPI ordering

<|begin_of_text|><|start_header_id|>system<|end_header_id|>

You are a Solana smart contract security analyzer. Analyze code for vulnerabilities according to OWASP Top 10 for Blockchain. Respond with the vulnerability status and details.<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>user<|end_header_id|>

You are a Solana smart contract security analyzer. Analyze the following Rust code for vulnerabilities.

Code:
pub fn process_recover_nested(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    let account_info_iter = &mut accounts.iter();
    let nested_associated_token_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let destination_associated_token_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let owner_associated_token_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let wallet_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let spl_token_program_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;

    let amount = nested_account.base.amount;
    let decimals = nested_mint.base.decimals;

    // Close the account FIRST — funds sent as rent to wallet
    invoke_signed(
        &spl_token_2022_interface::instruction::close_account(
            spl_token_program_id, nested_associated_token_account_info.key,
            wallet_account_info.key, owner_associated_token_account_info.key, &[],
        )?,
        &[nested_associated_token_account_info.clone(), wallet_account_info.clone(),
          owner_associated_token_account_info.clone()],
        &[owner_associated_token_account_signer_seeds],
    )?;

    // THEN transfer — but account is already closed!
    invoke_signed(
        &spl_token_2022_interface::instruction::transfer_checked(
            spl_token_program_id, nested_associated_token_account_info.key,
            nested_token_mint_info.key, destination_associated_token_account_info.key,
            owner_associated_token_account_info.key, &[], amount, decimals,
        )?,
        &[nested_associated_token_account_info.clone(), nested_token_mint_info.clone(),
          destination_associated_token_account_info.clone(), owner_associated_token_account_info.clone()],
        &[owner_associated_token_account_signer_seeds],
    )
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V5 - Reentrancy Attacks
CRITICAL
The CPI order is reversed: close_account executes before transfer_checked. The account is destroyed before tokens are transferred. The transfer will fail because the source account no longer exists, but the rent lamports from close have already been sent to the wallet. Tokens in the nested account are permanently lost.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - reversed CPI order: close_account executes before transfer_checked (original is transfer first, then close)

Code:
fn process_create_associated_token_account(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    create_mode: CreateMode,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let account_info_iter = &mut accounts.iter();
    let funder_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let associated_token_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let wallet_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let spl_token_mint_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let spl_token_program_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let spl_token_program_id = spl_token_program_info.key;

    let (associated_token_address, bump_seed) = get_associated_token_address_and_bump_seed_internal(
        wallet_account_info.key, spl_token_mint_info.key, program_id, spl_token_program_id,
    );
    if associated_token_address != *associated_token_account_info.key {
        return Err(ProgramError::InvalidSeeds);
    }

    if create_mode == CreateMode::Idempotent
        && associated_token_account_info.owner == spl_token_program_id
    {
        let ata_data = associated_token_account_info.data.borrow();
        if let Ok(associated_token_account) = StateWithExtensions::<Account>::unpack(&ata_data) {
            if associated_token_account.base.owner != *wallet_account_info.key {
                return Err(AssociatedTokenAccountError::InvalidOwner.into());
            }
            if associated_token_account.base.mint != *spl_token_mint_info.key {
                return Err(ProgramError::InvalidAccountData);
            }
            return Ok(());
        }
    }
    if *associated_token_account_info.owner != system_program::id() {
        return Err(ProgramError::IllegalOwner);
    }

    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V4 - Input Validation - MITIGATED
Idempotent mode properly validates existing accounts: checks owner matches wallet, mint matches expected mint, and account is owned by token program. Non-idempotent path verifies account is owned by system program (uninitialized). Prevents type confusion by validating account state before proceeding.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept idempotent validation logic

You are a Solana smart contract security analyzer. Analyze code for vulnerabilities according to OWASP Top 10 for Blockchain. Respond with the vulnerability status and details.<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>user<|end_header_id|>

You are a Solana smart contract security analyzer. Analyze the following Rust code for vulnerabilities.

Code:
pub fn authority_id(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    my_info: &Pubkey,
    bump_seed: u8,
) -> Result<Pubkey, ProgramError> {
    Pubkey::create_program_address(&[&my_info.to_bytes()[..32], &[bump_seed]], program_id)
        .map_err(|_| PoolError::InvalidAuthorityData.into())
}

pub fn process_init_pool(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    mint_end_slot: Slot,
    decide_end_slot: Slot,
    bump_seed: u8,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let account_info_iter = &mut accounts.iter();
    let pool_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let authority_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;

    let authority = Self::authority_id(program_id, pool_account_info.key, bump_seed)?;
    if *authority_info.key != authority {
        return Err(PoolError::InvalidAuthorityAccount.into());
    }

    let mut pool = Pool::try_from_slice(&pool_account_info.data.borrow())?;
    pool.bump_seed = bump_seed;
    pool.mint_end_slot = mint_end_slot;
    pool.decide_end_slot = decide_end_slot;
    
    borsh::to_writer(&mut pool_account_info.data.borrow_mut()[..], &pool)?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V9 - Bump Seed Canonicalization
HIGH
The authority_id function uses create_program_address() with a user-provided bump_seed from instruction data. Multiple bump values can produce valid PDAs for the same seeds. Should use find_program_address() to derive the canonical (highest valid) bump.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: direct extraction and simplified - this IS the real vulnerability: bump_seed comes from instruction data (line 190) and is used with create_program_address (line 34) instead of find_program_address

You are a Solana smart contract security analyzer. Analyze code for vulnerabilities according to OWASP Top 10 for Blockchain. Respond with the vulnerability status and details.<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>user<|end_header_id|>

You are a Solana smart contract security analyzer. Analyze the following Rust code for vulnerabilities.

Code:
pub fn authority_id(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    my_info: &Pubkey,
) -> Result<(Pubkey, u8), ProgramError> {
    Pubkey::find_program_address(&[&my_info.to_bytes()[..32]], program_id)
        .ok_or_else(|| PoolError::InvalidAuthorityData.into())
}

pub fn process_init_pool(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    mint_end_slot: Slot,
    decide_end_slot: Slot,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let account_info_iter = &mut accounts.iter();
    let pool_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let authority_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;

    let (authority, bump_seed) = Self::authority_id(program_id, pool_account_info.key)?;
    if *authority_info.key != authority {
        return Err(PoolError::InvalidAuthorityAccount.into());
    }

    let mut pool = Pool::try_from_slice(&pool_account_info.data.borrow())?;
    pool.bump_seed = bump_seed;
    pool.mint_end_slot = mint_end_slot;
    pool.decide_end_slot = decide_end_slot;
    
    borsh::to_writer(&mut pool_account_info.data.borrow_mut()[..], &pool)?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V9 - Bump Seed Canonicalization - MITIGATED
The authority_id function uses find_program_address() to derive the canonical bump. The bump_seed is no longer accepted from instruction data but derived internally. The canonical bump is stored in pool state for future operations.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - fixed the vulnerability: replaced create_program_address (L34) with find_program_address, removed user-provided bump_seed parameter (L190), bump is now derived canonically

You are a Solana smart contract security analyzer. Analyze the following Rust code for vulnerabilities.

Code:
pub fn mint<'a>(
    token_program_id: AccountInfo<'a>,
    mint_account: AccountInfo<'a>,
    destination_account: AccountInfo<'a>,
    authority_account: AccountInfo<'a>,
    amount: u64,
    pool_pub_key: &Pubkey,
    bump_seed: u8,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let me_bytes = pool_pub_key.to_bytes();
    let authority_signature_seeds = [&me_bytes[..32], &[bump_seed]];
    let signers = &[&authority_signature_seeds[..]];

    invoke_signed(
        &spl_token::instruction::mint_to(
            token_program_id.key,
            mint_account.key,
            destination_account.key,
            authority_account.key,
            &[authority_account.key],
            amount,
        )
        .unwrap(),
        &[token_program_id, mint_account, destination_account, authority_account],
        signers,
    )
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V6 - Unchecked External Calls
MEDIUM
The spl_token::instruction::mint_to() result is handled with .unwrap() instead of ?. If instruction creation fails (invalid program key, malformed parameters), the program panics instead of returning a clean error. While invoke_signed returns its result, the unwrap before it can cause unexpected program termination.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: direct extraction - the .unwrap() on instruction creation (line 117) is the vulnerability

pub fn mint<'a>(
    token_program_id: AccountInfo<'a>,
    mint_account: AccountInfo<'a>,
    destination_account: AccountInfo<'a>,
    authority_account: AccountInfo<'a>,
    amount: u64,
    pool_pub_key: &Pubkey,
    bump_seed: u8,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let me_bytes = pool_pub_key.to_bytes();
    let authority_signature_seeds = [&me_bytes[..32], &[bump_seed]];
    let signers = &[&authority_signature_seeds[..]];

    let ix = spl_token::instruction::mint_to(
        token_program_id.key,
        mint_account.key,
        destination_account.key,
        authority_account.key,
        &[authority_account.key],
        amount,
    )?;

    invoke_signed(
        &ix,
        &[token_program_id, mint_account, destination_account, authority_account],
        signers,
    )
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V6 - Unchecked External Calls - MITIGATED
The instruction creation uses ? for error propagation instead of .unwrap(). If mint_to instruction creation fails, the error is returned cleanly. The invoke_signed result is also returned, ensuring any CPI failure is propagated.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - replaced .unwrap() (L117) with ? for proper error propagation on instruction creation

Code:
pub fn transfer<'a>(
    token_program_id: AccountInfo<'a>,
    source_account: AccountInfo<'a>,
    destination_account: AccountInfo<'a>,
    program_authority_account: AccountInfo<'a>,
    user_authority_account: AccountInfo<'a>,
    amount: u64,
    pool_pub_key: &Pubkey,
    bump_seed: u8,
) -> ProgramResult {
    if program_authority_account.key == user_authority_account.key {
        let me_bytes = pool_pub_key.to_bytes();
        let authority_signature_seeds = [&me_bytes[..32], &[bump_seed]];
        let signers = &[&authority_signature_seeds[..]];

        invoke_signed(
            &spl_token::instruction::transfer(
                token_program_id.key,
                source_account.key,
                destination_account.key,
                program_authority_account.key,
                &[program_authority_account.key],
                amount,
            )
            .unwrap(),
            &[token_program_id, program_authority_account, source_account, destination_account],
            signers,
        )
    } else {
        invoke(
            &spl_token::instruction::transfer(
                token_program_id.key,
                source_account.key,
                destination_account.key,
                user_authority_account.key,
                &[user_authority_account.key],
                amount,
            )
            .unwrap(),
            &[token_program_id, user_authority_account, source_account, destination_account],
        )
    }
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V6 - Unchecked External Calls
MEDIUM
Both code paths (program authority and user authority) use .unwrap() on spl_token::instruction::transfer(). If instruction creation fails with invalid parameters, the program panics instead of returning a recoverable error.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: direct extraction - both code paths use .unwrap() on instruction creation (lines 64, 83)

Code:
pub fn process_deposit(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    amount: u64,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let account_info_iter = &mut accounts.iter();
    let pool_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let authority_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let user_transfer_authority_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let user_token_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let pool_deposit_token_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let token_pass_mint_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let token_fail_mint_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let token_pass_destination_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let token_fail_destination_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let clock = &Clock::from_account_info(next_account_info(account_info_iter)?)?;
    let token_program_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;

    if amount == 0 {
        return Err(PoolError::InvalidAmount.into());
    }

    let pool = Pool::try_from_slice(&pool_account_info.data.borrow())?;
    if clock.slot > pool.mint_end_slot {
        return Err(PoolError::InvalidSlotForDeposit.into());
    }

    let authority_pub_key = Self::authority_id(program_id, pool_account_info.key, pool.bump_seed)?;
    if *authority_account_info.key != authority_pub_key {
        return Err(PoolError::InvalidAuthorityAccount.into());
    }

    // FIRST: Take user's deposit tokens
    Self::transfer(
        token_program_info.clone(), user_token_account_info.clone(),
        pool_deposit_token_account_info.clone(), authority_account_info.clone(),
        user_transfer_authority_info.clone(), amount, pool_account_info.key, pool.bump_seed,
    )?;

    // THEN: Mint PASS and FAIL tokens to user
    Self::mint(
        token_program_info.clone(), token_pass_mint_info.clone(),
        token_pass_destination_info.clone(), authority_account_info.clone(),
        amount, pool_account_info.key, pool.bump_seed,
    )?;
    Self::mint(
        token_program_info.clone(), token_fail_mint_info.clone(),
        token_fail_destination_info.clone(), authority_account_info.clone(),
        amount, pool_account_info.key, pool.bump_seed,
    )?;

    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V5 - Reentrancy Attacks - MITIGATED
Correct CPI ordering: user's deposit tokens are transferred to the pool FIRST, then PASS and FAIL tokens are minted. If reentrancy occurs during mint, the user's deposit has already been taken. The user cannot deposit the same tokens twice.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: direct extraction

Code:
pub fn process_deposit(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    amount: u64,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let account_info_iter = &mut accounts.iter();
    let pool_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let authority_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let user_transfer_authority_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let user_token_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let pool_deposit_token_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let token_pass_mint_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let token_fail_mint_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let token_pass_destination_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let token_fail_destination_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let token_program_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;

    let pool = Pool::try_from_slice(&pool_account_info.data.borrow())?;

    // WRONG: Mint tokens FIRST before taking deposit
    Self::mint(
        token_program_info.clone(), token_pass_mint_info.clone(),
        token_pass_destination_info.clone(), authority_account_info.clone(),
        amount, pool_account_info.key, pool.bump_seed,
    )?;
    Self::mint(
        token_program_info.clone(), token_fail_mint_info.clone(),
        token_fail_destination_info.clone(), authority_account_info.clone(),
        amount, pool_account_info.key, pool.bump_seed,
    )?;

    // THEN take deposit - but user already has PASS/FAIL tokens
    Self::transfer(
        token_program_info.clone(), user_token_account_info.clone(),
        pool_deposit_token_account_info.clone(), authority_account_info.clone(),
        user_transfer_authority_info.clone(), amount, pool_account_info.key, pool.bump_seed,
    )?;

    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V5 - Reentrancy Attacks
CRITICAL
CPI order is reversed: PASS and FAIL tokens are minted to the user before their deposit is taken. During the mint CPI callback, an attacker can use the newly minted tokens to withdraw from the pool before the deposit transfer completes. The attacker gets pool tokens without actually depositing.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - reversed CPI order: mint PASS/FAIL tokens before taking user's deposit (original takes deposit first at line 344, then mints at lines 356-374)

pub fn process_withdraw(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    amount: u64,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let account_info_iter = &mut accounts.iter();
    let pool_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let authority_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let user_transfer_authority_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let pool_deposit_token_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let token_pass_user_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let token_pass_mint_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let user_token_destination_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let token_program_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;

    if amount == 0 {
        return Err(PoolError::InvalidAmount.into());
    }

    let pool = Pool::try_from_slice(&pool_account_info.data.borrow())?;
    if pool.token_pass_mint != *token_pass_mint_info.key {
        return Err(PoolError::InvalidTokenMint.into());
    }

    let authority_pub_key = Self::authority_id(program_id, pool_account_info.key, pool.bump_seed)?;
    if *authority_account_info.key != authority_pub_key {
        return Err(PoolError::InvalidAuthorityAccount.into());
    }

    // FIRST: Burn user's PASS tokens (destroys withdrawal authorization)
    Self::burn(
        token_program_info.clone(), token_pass_user_account_info.clone(),
        token_pass_mint_info.clone(), authority_account_info.clone(),
        user_transfer_authority_info.clone(), amount, pool_account_info.key, pool.bump_seed,
    )?;

    // THEN: Transfer deposit tokens back to user
    Self::transfer(
        token_program_info.clone(), pool_deposit_token_account_info.clone(),
        user_token_destination_info.clone(), authority_account_info.clone(),
        authority_account_info.clone(), amount, pool_account_info.key, pool.bump_seed,
    )?;

    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V5 - Reentrancy Attacks - MITIGATED
Correct CEI pattern: PASS tokens are burned FIRST, destroying the user's withdrawal authorization. Then deposit tokens are transferred back. Any reentrancy during the transfer would fail because the user no longer has PASS tokens to burn again.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept burn-then-transfer pattern from Decision::Pass branch (lines 421-444)

pub fn process_withdraw(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    amount: u64,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let account_info_iter = &mut accounts.iter();
    let pool_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let authority_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let user_transfer_authority_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let pool_deposit_token_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let token_pass_user_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let token_pass_mint_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let user_token_destination_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let token_program_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;

    let pool = Pool::try_from_slice(&pool_account_info.data.borrow())?;

    let authority_pub_key = Self::authority_id(program_id, pool_account_info.key, pool.bump_seed)?;
    if *authority_account_info.key != authority_pub_key {
        return Err(PoolError::InvalidAuthorityAccount.into());
    }

    // WRONG: Transfer deposit tokens FIRST
    Self::transfer(
        token_program_info.clone(), pool_deposit_token_account_info.clone(),
        user_token_destination_info.clone(), authority_account_info.clone(),
        authority_account_info.clone(), amount, pool_account_info.key, pool.bump_seed,
    )?;

    // THEN burn — but user already has the deposit tokens
    Self::burn(
        token_program_info.clone(), token_pass_user_account_info.clone(),
        token_pass_mint_info.clone(), authority_account_info.clone(),
        user_transfer_authority_info.clone(), amount, pool_account_info.key, pool.bump_seed,
    )?;

    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V5 - Reentrancy Attacks
CRITICAL
Deposit tokens are transferred to the user before PASS tokens are burned. During the transfer CPI callback, an attacker can recursively call withdraw() with the same PASS tokens. Since PASS tokens haven't been burned yet, each call passes validation and drains the pool's deposit account.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - reversed CPI order: transfer executes before burn (original burns first at L423-432, then transfers at L435-444)

You are a Solana smart contract security analyzer. Analyze the following Rust code for vulnerabilities.

Code:
pub fn process_decide(
    _program_id: &Pubkey,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    decision: bool,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let account_info_iter = &mut accounts.iter();
    let pool_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let decider_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let clock_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let clock = &Clock::from_account_info(clock_info)?;

    let mut pool = Pool::try_from_slice(&pool_account_info.data.borrow())?;

    if *decider_account_info.key != pool.decider {
        return Err(PoolError::WrongDeciderAccount.into());
    }

    if !decider_account_info.is_signer {
        return Err(PoolError::SignatureMissing.into());
    }

    if pool.decision != Decision::Undecided {
        return Err(PoolError::DecisionAlreadyMade.into());
    }

    let current_slot = clock.slot;
    if current_slot < pool.mint_end_slot || current_slot > pool.decide_end_slot {
        return Err(PoolError::InvalidSlotForDecision.into());
    }

    pool.decision = if decision { Decision::Pass } else { Decision::Fail };
    borsh::to_writer(&mut pool_account_info.data.borrow_mut()[..], &pool)?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V1 - Access Control - MITIGATED
The function validates: (1) decider_account_info matches pool.decider stored in state, (2) decider must be a signer, (3) decision hasn't been made yet (prevents re-decision), (4) current slot is within decision window. Complete authorization chain.<|eot_id|>

pub fn process_decide(
    _program_id: &Pubkey,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    decision: bool,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let account_info_iter = &mut accounts.iter();
    let pool_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let decider_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let clock_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let clock = &Clock::from_account_info(clock_info)?;

    let mut pool = Pool::try_from_slice(&pool_account_info.data.borrow())?;

    if pool.decision != Decision::Undecided {
        return Err(PoolError::DecisionAlreadyMade.into());
    }

    pool.decision = if decision { Decision::Pass } else { Decision::Fail };
    borsh::to_writer(&mut pool_account_info.data.borrow_mut()[..], &pool)?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V1 - Access Control
CRITICAL
The function does not verify that decider_account_info is the authorized decider stored in pool.decider, and does not require a signature. Anyone can decide the pool outcome, allowing an attacker to manipulate which token holders (PASS vs FAIL) receive the deposit.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

Code:
pub fn process_withdraw(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    amount: u64,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let account_info_iter = &mut accounts.iter();
    let pool_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let authority_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let user_transfer_authority_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let pool_deposit_token_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let token_pass_user_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let token_fail_user_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let token_pass_mint_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let token_fail_mint_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let user_token_destination_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let token_program_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;

    let user_pass_token_account = Account::unpack(&token_pass_user_account_info.data.borrow())?;
    let user_fail_token_account = Account::unpack(&token_fail_user_account_info.data.borrow())?;
    let pool = Pool::try_from_slice(&pool_account_info.data.borrow())?;

    let possible_withdraw_amount = amount
        .min(user_pass_token_account.amount)
        .min(user_fail_token_account.amount);

    // Burns PASS tokens using min amount
    Self::burn(token_program_info.clone(), token_pass_user_account_info.clone(),
        token_pass_mint_info.clone(), authority_account_info.clone(),
        user_transfer_authority_info.clone(), possible_withdraw_amount,
        pool_account_info.key, pool.bump_seed,
    )?;

    // BUG: Burns FAIL tokens using original amount, not possible_withdraw_amount
    Self::burn(token_program_info.clone(), token_fail_user_account_info.clone(),
        token_fail_mint_info.clone(), authority_account_info.clone(),
        user_transfer_authority_info.clone(), amount,
        pool_account_info.key, pool.bump_seed,
    )?;

    // Transfers original amount, not possible_withdraw_amount
    Self::transfer(token_program_info.clone(), pool_deposit_token_account_info.clone(),
        user_token_destination_info.clone(), authority_account_info.clone(),
        authority_account_info.clone(), amount,
        pool_account_info.key, pool.bump_seed,
    )?;

    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V4 - Input Validation
HIGH
Inconsistent amount handling in undecided withdrawal: PASS tokens are burned using min(amount, pass_balance, fail_balance) but FAIL tokens are burned using the original amount. If user has fewer FAIL tokens than PASS tokens, the FAIL burn will fail. The transfer also uses the original amount rather than the adjusted amount, potentially over-withdrawing from the pool.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

Code:
pub fn process_deposit(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    amount: u64,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let account_info_iter = &mut accounts.iter();
    let pool_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let authority_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let clock = &Clock::from_account_info(next_account_info(account_info_iter)?)?;

    if amount == 0 {
        return Err(PoolError::InvalidAmount.into());
    }

    let pool = Pool::try_from_slice(&pool_account_info.data.borrow())?;

    if clock.slot > pool.mint_end_slot {
        return Err(PoolError::InvalidSlotForDeposit.into());
    }

    let authority_pub_key = Self::authority_id(program_id, pool_account_info.key, pool.bump_seed)?;
    if *authority_account_info.key != authority_pub_key {
        return Err(PoolError::InvalidAuthorityAccount.into());
    }

    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V4 - Input Validation - MITIGATED
The function validates: zero amount rejection, deposit slot window check (must be before mint_end_slot), and authority verification. Prevents meaningless zero deposits and out-of-window deposits.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept validation logic: zero check (L327-329), slot check (L333-335), authority check (L337-341)
